records.ety: [AS. race; akin to OD. rake, D. reek, OHG. rehho, G. rechen, Icel. reka a shovel, and to Goth. rikan to heap up, collect, and perhaps to Gr. 'ore`gein to stretch out, and E. rack to stretch. Cf. Reckon.]

defs: An implement consisting of a headpiece having teeth, and a long handle at right angles to it, -- used for collecting hay, or other light things which are spread over a large surface, or for breaking and smoothing the earth.
A toothed machine drawn by a horse, -- used for collecting hay or grain; a horserake.
A fissure or mineral vein traversing the strata vertically, or nearly so; -- called also rake-vein.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Raked (rākt); p. pr. & vb. n. Raking.]

defs: To collect with a rake; as, to rake hay; -- often with up; as, he raked up the fallen leaves.

To collect or draw together with laborious industry; to gather from a wide space; to scrape together; as, to rake together wealth; to rake together slanderous tales; to rake together the rabble of a town.
To pass a rake over; to scrape or scratch with a rake for the purpose of collecting and clearing off something, or for stirring up the soil; as, to rake a lawn; to rake a flower bed.
To search through; to scour; to ransack.
quotes: The statesman rakes the town to find a plot.

defs: To scrape or scratch across; to pass over quickly and lightly, as a rake does.
quotes: Like clouds that rake the mountain summits.

defs: To enfilade; to fire in a direction with the length of; in naval engagements, to cannonade, as a ship, on the stern or head so that the balls range the whole length of the deck.
To use a rake, as for searching or for collecting; to scrape; to search minutely.
quotes: One is for raking in Chaucer for antiquated words.

defs: To pass with violence or rapidity; to scrape along.
quotes: Pas could not stay, but over him did rake.

records.ety: [Cf. dial. Sw. raka to reach, and E. reach.]

defs: The inclination of anything from a perpendicular direction; as, the rake of a roof, a staircase, etc.
the inclination of a mast or funnel, or, in general, of any part of a vessel not perpendicular to the keel.
To incline from a perpendicular direction; as, a mast rakes aft.

records.ety: [OE. rakel rash; cf. Icel. reikall wandering, unsettled, reika to wander.]

defs: A loose, disorderly, vicious man; a person addicted to lewdness and other scandalous vices; a debauchee; a rou.
quotes: An illiterate and frivolous old rake.

defs: To walk about; to gad or ramble idly.
To act the rake; to lead a dissolute, debauched life.
